Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}{{jalink|Sōja|Q873536}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |社格 = {{jalink|small Shikinaisha|Q134917287}}, former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Sōja|Q873536}}, {{jalink|Okayama Prefecture|Q132936}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (small) of {{jalink|Shimomichi District|Q10868734}}, {{jalink|Bitchū Province|Q1047514}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{jalink|Masakiyama}}, and its {{jalink|ujiko|Q125562175}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{jalink|Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|Prince Ōtomo}} (son of {{jalink|Emperor Tenji|Q314802}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{jalink|Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{jalink|stone monument|Q178743}} (built {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 3 / {{jalink|1836|Q7601}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, then was revived in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 13 ({{jalink|1585|Q6736}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}} 10 ({{jalink|1827|Q7008}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{jalink|Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)||Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}. === Festivals === *{{jalink|April 29|Q2535}} - Spring Festival *{{jalink|August 4|Q2784}} - Summer Festival *{{jalink|October 21|Q2960}} and {{jalink|October 22|Q2957|22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{jalink|Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313}} is dedicated in the {{jalink|kaguraden|Q94498}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{jalink|Bunsei|Q1327119}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}}, hira-iri. A southern {{jalink|corridor|Q106426877}} links it to the {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{jalink|shamusho}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{jalink|bell tower|Q1363585}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{jalink|Sōja Station|Q844657}} on JR {{jalink|Hakubi Line|Q844666}}, JR {{jalink|Kibi Line|Q844682}}, or {{jalink|Ibara Railway|Q11373397}}, take a {{jalink|Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772}} bound for {{jalink|Niimoto}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{jalink|Kibi Road|Q11412656}} *{{jalink|Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{jalink|World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{jalink|Tenpuku Zenji}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{jalink|Daiki Zenji}}, active in the {{jalink|Sengoku period|Q204023}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill||lt=Masakiyama|UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill||lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)||WD=|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill||lt=shamusho|UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772}} bound for {{ill||lt=Niimoto|UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill||lt=Tenpuku Zenji|UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill||lt=Daiki Zenji|UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)||WD=|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|1=UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)||WD=|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|1=UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|1=UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|1=UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|1=UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|1=UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|1=UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|1=UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|1=UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|1=UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734|1=Katō district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo|1=Emperor Kōbun}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|1=UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743|1=Stele}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601|1=1836}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008|1=1827}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784|1=August 4}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22|1=October 22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313|1=Bichu Kagura}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877|1=corridor}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|1=UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585|1=Shōrō}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657|1=Sōja Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666|1=Hakubi Line}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682|1=Kibi Line}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397|1=Ibara Railway}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772|1=Chutetsu Bus}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656|1=Kibiji District}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230|1=Mamoru Kiguchi}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551|1=World Co.}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734|1=Katō district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo|1=Emperor Kōbun}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|1=UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743|1=Stele}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601|1=1836}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008|1=1827}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784|1=August 4}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22|1=October 22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313|1=Bichu Kagura}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877|1=corridor}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|1=UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585|1=Shōrō}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657|1=Sōja Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666|1=Hakubi Line}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682|1=Kibi Line}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397|1=Ibara Railway}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772|1=Chutetsu Bus}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656|1=Kibiji District}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230|1=Mamoru Kiguchi}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551|1=World Co.}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4260005004727 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県総社市久代3632', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:横田神社 (総社市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11543413}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yokota Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yokota Jinja, Okayama 12-06-03.JPG|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}3632 Kushiro |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|38.0|N|133|40|52.7|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 29 (spring festival)
August 4 (summer festival)
October 21 and 22 (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Yokota Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yokota Shrine''' (よこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sōja|Q873536|WD=Q873536|1=Sōja}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is one of the seven shrines in Sōja counted as Engishiki-listed shrines, being a {{ill|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small) of {{ill|lt=Shimomichi District|Q10868734|WD=Q10868734|1=Katō district}}, {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}. It stands in the Kushiro area in western Sōja, on the southern foot of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakiyama|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} area covers central and northwestern parts of that district (koaza names: Bessho, Yokota, Origami, Yamaguchi, Shimizu, Oshikida). Former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, with divine virtues such as academic success and wish fulfillment. === Origin === Founding era is unknown. One tradition says that when {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} stayed at a temporary palace here, {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtomo|Q357242|WD=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo|1=Emperor Kōbun}} (son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}) attacked with a great army to overthrow him. The emperor purified himself, worshiped heaven and earth deities, prayed for victory, defeated Prince Ōtomo's army, then built and revered a small shrine, which became Yokota Shrine'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku|1=UNKNOWN}}' quoting 'Kibi District Shrine Record.'. According to a precinct {{ill|lt=stone monument|Q178743|WD=Q178743|1=Stele}} (built {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 3 / {{ill|lt=1836|Q7601|WD=Q7601|1=1836}}) and other materials, the shrine declined once in the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, then was revived in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}}) as '''Tenjingū'''. Later it was redeveloped between around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1827|Q7008|WD=Q7008|1=1827}}) and Tenpō 7 (1836), and renamed again as '''Yokota Shrine'''. The current buildings are from around that period. Formerly, {{ill|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji (Sōja)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōrinzan Shōfuku-ji}} in Bessho, west of the shrine site, served as the shrine's {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. === Festivals === *{{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} - Spring Festival *{{ill|lt=August 4|Q2784|WD=Q2784|1=August 4}} - Summer Festival *{{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} and {{ill|lt=October 22|Q2957|WD=Q2957|lt=22|1=October 22}} - Autumn Festival (with dedication of Bitchū Kagura) Three festivals are held yearly (spring, summer, autumn); the autumn one is especially large, and {{ill|lt=Bitchū Kagura|Q11387313|WD=Q11387313|1=Bichu Kagura}} is dedicated in the {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}} described below. === Shrine buildings === Current shrine buildings were completed around {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 10. The honden is 1-ken wide and 2-ken deep, {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, hira-iri. A southern {{ill|lt=corridor|Q106426877|WD=Q106426877|1=corridor}} links it to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. Corridors extend east, west, and south from the haiden; especially the south corridor is long, and from above the complex appears in a distinct cross-like plan. West of the haiden is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shamusho|1=UNKNOWN}}, east is Tenjingū enshrining Sugawara no Michizane, and at the end of the southern corridor stands a zuijin gate. === Precinct === Unusually for a shrine, it has a {{ill|lt=bell tower|Q1363585|WD=Q1363585|1=Shōrō}}. A kaguraden is also built on the south side of the precinct. Many small precinct shrines are enshrined within the grounds. === Access === From {{ill|lt=Sōja Station|Q844657|WD=Q844657|1=Sōja Station}} on JR {{ill|lt=Hakubi Line|Q844666|WD=Q844666|1=Hakubi Line}}, JR {{ill|lt=Kibi Line|Q844682|WD=Q844682|1=Kibi Line}}, or {{ill|lt=Ibara Railway|Q11373397|WD=Q11373397|1=Ibara Railway}}, take a {{ill|lt=Chūtetsu Bus|Q11367772|WD=Q11367772|1=Chutetsu Bus}} bound for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niimoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; get off at Yokota stop and walk about 3 minutes. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' vol.22 Sanyōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, 1980 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Kibi Road|Q11412656|WD=Q11412656|1=Kibiji District}} *{{ill|lt=Mamoru Kiguchi|Q11518230|WD=Q11518230|1=Mamoru Kiguchi}} - raised as ujiko of this shrine; founder of {{ill|lt=World Co., Ltd.|Q8035551|WD=Q8035551|1=World Co.}}. Continued donations and worship after founding his company; a stone monument in the precinct records this. *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenpuku Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}} - the area around this shrine is called Yokota and is both birthplace and final place of Zen monk {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daiki Zenji|1=UNKNOWN}}, active in the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}. The temple north of this shrine is where he trained in childhood. === External links === *[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19026 Yokota Shrine] (Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:よこたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:備中国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧村社]] [[Category:総社市の建築物]] [[Category:総社市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QYokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Yokota Shrine (Q11543413)}}[[Category:qqq]]
